rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: JOHN/JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner John/Jane Doe'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 1 Petitioner John/Jane Doe's motion to quash subpoena duces tecum and for sanctions is CONTINUED to September 6, 2019 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to allow the Illinois court to rule on the motions to quash and intervene that are pending before it. Movant's status update indicates that the hearing in Illinois has been canceled, but that it is expected to be reset some time in August 2019. The parties are instructed to advise this court of the Illinois court's rulings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX. REL RON KENNEDY M.D. ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 2 RESPONDENTS ALEXIS PODESTA and DEAN GRAFILO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION is OFF CALENDAR. CCP §430.41 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed. Counsel's declaration demonstrates that has not occurred, so the demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR to allow compliance with §430.41. Defendants have until July 28, 2019 to respond to the amended complaint after the meet and confer efforts. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. PETER M. RADIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 6 DEFENDANT PETER RADIN'S Motion To Vacate Judgment DENIED. Even assuming the motion was timely and the form LLP-4 effective and admissible, the form would not help defendant. Rather, the most it could show is that plaintiff lost its status as a limited liability partnership. (Carolina Casualty Ins. Co. v. L.M. Ross Law Group, LLP (2012) 212 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1194-95.) The LLP thus reverted to a general partnership which could pursue its debt against defendant. (James F. Fotenos and Edward C. Rybka, California Practice Guide-Pass -Through Entities (TRG 2019) §4:290; Corps. Code §§16954(b)(d)(e), 16802(a),16803(a)(c).) However, the complaint and its attached statement of account provided no date on which payments became due, so defendant was not notified of the amount of prejudgment interest sought, beyond that calculated as of October 1, 2014. (Electronic Funds Solutions, LLC v. Murphy (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1161, 1174 ("purpose of [CCP] section 580 is to require the plaintiff to provide notice of the maximum amount of the defendant's potential liability"); Stein v. York (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 320, 325 ("Section 580 must be strictly construed").) Thus, $2,280.82 awarded as interest is stricken from the judgment; the judgment otherwise stands. (See Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1209.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALLISON LESHEFSKY VS. RENKA PROP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And Proposed Order For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: June 28, 2019 Line 1, 3) PLAINTIFF ALLISON LESHEFSKY'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And Proposed Order For Sanctions Pro Tem Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's unopposed motion to deem the requests for admissions admitted is granted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to conormoore@fdic.gov with a copy to all other parties by 4pm, stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. (JPT/RBU/302).

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION, ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 14 PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION's Motion For Attorneys Fees DENIED. As to Code of Civil Procedure §1021.5, my rulings in this matter did not confer a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons. Rather, as indicated in my written order and at oral argument, the benefit is specific to Deputy Douglas Jones. As to Government Code §800, I find that the City's conduct did not quite descend to the level of being arbitrary or capricious. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: UNITED CALIFORNIA GLASS & DOOR VS. CARLO DOYLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 15 PLAINTIFF UNITED CALIFORNIA GLASS & DOOR'S Motion To Amend Complaint GRANTED. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428.) Moreover, this case was only filed 16 months ago and can be brought to trial expeditiously. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY SHMIGELSKY VS. GETAROUND, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Bond Pursuant To Corporations Code Section 800
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 28, 2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LINE 13 DEFENDANT GETAROUND, INC.'S Motion For Bond Pursuant To Corporations Code Section 800 DENIED. The Court may only grant motions for bonds in shareholder derivative actions. (Corp. Code § 800(b), (c), (d).) Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky does not assert a shareholder derivative claim and his first amended complaint does not allege "continuous stock ownership throughout the pendency of litigation" as required under California law. (Grosset v. Wenaas (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1100, 1119.) Therefore, Corporations Code section 800 is inapplicable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/RBU)
